reasoning: Johannes Kaiser is a controversial deputy who left the Republican Party. The Chilean right is currently dominated by Evelyn Matthei (UDI) and José Antonio Kast (Republican Party). Early polling for the 2025 election shows Kaiser in the low single digits (1-3%). To win the first round, he would need to surpass both established right-wing leaders and the main left/center-left candidate, which is highly improbable without major institutional support or a complete collapse of frontrunners. His polling and political positioning make a first-round victory exceptionally unlikely.
